name: La Cite du Vin
summary: Bordeaux's signature wine-culture museum, best for a polished indoor stop tied to the region's main identity.
whyItMatters: The Bordeaux stage and Saint-Emilion area make wine culture central to this part of the trip; this is the highest-profile city version of that theme.
ticketOrReservationNeed: Ticket recommended; timed entry may be useful in July.
pros: Directly relevant to Bordeaux and the wider wine itinerary
Indoor, structured, and easy to plan around heat or rain
Accessible by Bordeaux tram in normal conditions
cons: Not as short as a 30-minute photo stop
Can feel less intimate than Saint-Emilion or a winery visit
Race-day transit or finish-area crowds may affect timing

logistics.opening_hours: Official site should be checked for July 2026 schedule and ticket slots.
logistics.distance_from_base: Bordeaux lodging dependent; north of the historic center.
logistics.distance_from_viewing_point: Not at the Stage 7 finish; requires tram/taxi/walk planning.
logistics.transit: Bordeaux tram Line B normally serves Cite du Vin; verify race-day transit notices.
logistics.road_closure_notes: Stage 7 finish in Bordeaux may affect central transit and roads.

timing.best_visit_window: Bordeaux non-race day or Stage 7 morning if finish logistics are already secured.

nearbyFoodDrinkPairing: Pair with Chartrons wine bars or riverfront food before/after, depending on transit.

sources.title: La Cite du Vin official site
Cite du Vin - Wikipedia
sources.note: Secondary support for the museum's wine-culture purpose, Bordeaux location, opening history, and tram access context.

open_questions: Is the group already planning a winery-heavy Saint-Emilion day, making this redundant?
What does Bordeaux transit look like on Stage 7 finish day?
